rule: Every application result names the next field decision, links to at least one relevant editorial or application route, and provides a clear return to Start Here. All routes must be source-controlled and non-placeholder.

applications.name: Compatibility Builder
applications.resultPrompt: Use the weakest link to choose the next setup or field test.

continuations.label: Open setup cards and printable field resources
Choose a presentation test
Read the equipment and field-file system
Return to Start Here

applications.name: Hatch Match
applications.resultPrompt: Treat the candidate as a hypothesis and decide what visible cue or presentation variable to test next.

continuations.label: Turn the hypothesis into a bounded presentation test
Read species, water, and conditions field files
Inspect source and uncertainty standards
Return to Start Here

applications.name: Presentation Planner
applications.resultPrompt: Confirm the setup, verify current conditions, and change one major variable during the field test.

continuations.label: Check whether the complete setup works together
Verify current trip conditions and access
Open supporting field files
Return to Start Here

applications.name: Rig Signal
applications.resultPrompt: Keep the event within its evidence limits before using it in a field decision.

continuations.label: Open the field systems studio
Read the field-testing and evidence system
Inspect source, privacy, and evidence standards
Return to Start Here

applications.name: Honey Hole Intelligence
applications.resultPrompt: Resolve blocked official facts first; when the trip remains responsible, choose the smallest field test and keep a public-safe fallback.

continuations.label: Build a presentation test from owned inventory
Read conditions, destination, and trip field files
Inspect current-source and location-protection standards
Return to Start Here
